Who Will Go From Spokane to the
Chicago Conference and Bring
Back the Concessions Which the
Railroads Are Anxious to Maker
mot l." Says Mr.. Schiller" mot
l." Says Mr.. Gooda||."

pages.raw_text:
who is going to attend the Chicago
meeting and represent the Spokane
shippers and argue their case? This
question was propounded to a num-
Der of Spokane merchants today, but
provoked no answer. No meeting Of
the shippers association has occurred
since the memorable one when the
jobbers betrayed the retailers and de-
clared the boycott off. No one knows
whether the association is still in ex-
istence. Its former president says he
is taking no interest in it's affairs and
is not going to Chicago and does not
know who is. President Schiller
fought for the continuance of the
boycott and says he is still shipping
over the o. r. & ~, and will con-
tinue to do so regardless Of the
others. but that he will have nothing
to do with the Chicago meeting.


From all that can be learned by
representatives of this paper only
those will go to Chicago who have
personal grievances which they think
will be remedied because they were
included among the friends" of the
railroads mentioned in Vice Presi-
dent Blabon's famous congratulatory
message to Jackson of the Great
Northern. From present indications
